evals.prompt: I need a landing page for my SaaS. It is a time-tracking app for remote engineering teams. We integrate with Jira, GitHub, and Slack. Our differentiator is that we automatically categorize time by project and client using AI, so managers get reports without anyone filling timesheets. Target audience: engineering managers at 50-500 person remote-first companies. I want it to feel calm and professional, not another loud productivity tool. I need it live in 3 weeks. No compliance requirements. English only for now. I have a logo and 3 product screenshots.
evals.expected_output: The agent follows the 11-stage running order from SKILL.md. It asks discovery questions one at a time, produces brief.md and constraints.md, then brand.md, then sitemap + wireframes, then copy, then tokens, then stack, then scaffold, then pages, then tests, then audits, then launch.md. It applies non-negotiables without being asked (img width/height/alt, WCAG contrast, font-display, prefers-reduced-motion, native HTML first, no positive tabindex, two-space indent). It does not dump all 16 chapters at once.

evals.prompt: Make my pricing page accessible and fix the color contrast. The page has three tiers: Starter ($29/mo), Pro ($79/mo), Enterprise (custom). The Pro card has a 'Most Popular' badge. Currently the badge text is white on a light orange (#FFA500) background, and the disabled 'Current Plan' button uses a light gray (#CCCCCC) background with white text. The page uses a serif font for headings and a sans-serif for body. There is a comparison table with 12 features. The page is built in Next.js with Tailwind.
evals.expected_output: The agent loads 05-accessibility.md and 03-visual-design.md. It identifies the contrast failures (white on #FFA500, white on #CCCCCC), proposes accessible color alternatives using OKLCH or hex that meet WCAG 2.2 AA (4.5:1 for body, 3:1 for large text/UI), restructures the comparison table with proper semantic HTML (scope, headers, captions), checks heading hierarchy, ensures keyboard navigation through the pricing cards, verifies focus-visible styles, audits the 'Most Popular' badge for color-not-alone meaning, and writes a concise report with severity and fix order. It does not spontaneously scaffold a full repo.

The agent loads 06-performance.md, 07-javascript-and-html.md, and 09-anti-patterns-and-process.md. It identifies: (1) new array reference in useEffect causing re-sort every 2s, (2) inline onAddToCart function causing ProductCard re-render every parent render, (3) grid-cols-3 without responsive breakpoints hurting mobile, (4) missing memoization (useMemo for sorting, React.memo for ProductCard or useCallback for handler), (5) no virtualization for 500 items (windowing). It proposes concrete fixes in order of impact, mentions INP and CLS considerations, and suggests measuring before/after with web-vitals. It does not rewrite the entire app or add unnecessary dependencies.

evals.prompt: Audit and rewrite the hero copy for this landing page. The product is a CI/CD observability tool for engineering teams. Current hero copy: "Empower your DevOps team to streamline deployments and unlock the full potential of your pipeline. Our robust platform leverages AI to foster seamless collaboration, ensuring optimized workflows that drive innovation at scale." The target audience is senior platform engineers at Series B–D startups. Brand voice: direct, but not curt; technical, but not jargon-heavy; wry, but not snarky; senior, but not condescending. Archetype: Sage primary, Hero secondary.
evals.expected_output: The agent loads 02-brand-and-copywriting.md. It identifies AI-sounding patterns in the current copy: abstract verbs (empower, unlock, leverage, foster, drive), vague collectives (full potential, seamless collaboration, optimized workflows, innovation at scale), no specific audience named, no concrete mechanism, no numbers, hedging tone. It proposes rewritten copy using the headline formulas, hero section patterns, and power-word rules from chapter 02. The rewrite names the specific audience (senior platform engineers), uses concrete verbs (ship, debug, page, deploy), includes a specific mechanism or number if available, avoids 'delve', 'landscape', 'robust', 'holistic', 'seamless', 'foster', 'leverage', 'navigate', 'tapestry', 'multifaceted', and follows the brand voice with 'but not' modifiers. It does not produce generic 'AI-powered' or 'revolutionary' claims.

evals.prompt: Write a transactional email for a SaaS product. The user just downgraded from Pro to Free. The product is an error-tracking tool (Flint — groups errors by root cause, not stack frame). Brand voice: direct, but not curt; technical, but not jargon-heavy; wry, but not snarky; senior, but not condescending. Tone for churn context: mid formality, warm, low urgency, no play; never guilt.
evals.expected_output: The agent loads 02-brand-and-copywriting.md. It follows the transactional email rules (subject states outcome, one action per email, signed from a named human for relationship-sensitive emails). It follows the tone ladder for churn/cancellation: mid formality, warm, low urgency, no play, never guilt. The subject line states the outcome in 30–50 characters. The body leads with the reason the email was sent. It names the specific change (Pro → Free), states what ends and when, preserves the user's data, offers a single action (resubscribe or contact support), and avoids guilt language ('We hate to see you go', 'Are you sure?'). It uses the brand's vocabulary (root cause, signal, noise, on-call, ship, incident) and avoids forbidden words (unleash, empower, revolutionize, AI-powered, game-changer).

evals.prompt:
Rewrite this copy using the anti-AI checklist in chapter 02. Product: error-tracking SaaS (Flint). Audience: senior platform engineers. Brand: direct-but-not-curt, technical-but-not-jargon, wry-but-not-snarky, senior-but-not-condescending.

Current copy: "Leveraging our robust platform, engineering teams can streamline their deployment pipeline, fostering seamless collaboration and unlocking the full potential of their workflow. By implementing the optimization of error grouping, teams can significantly reduce noise while ensuring comprehensive visibility into root cause analysis.\
evals.expected_output: The agent loads 02-brand-and-copywriting.md and applies the AI copy DNA section. It identifies: (1) present participial overload — 'Leveraging... fostering... unlocking' (pattern 12), (2) nominalization chains — 'implementation of the optimization' (pattern 13), (3) vague collectives — 'robust platform', 'seamless collaboration', 'full potential', 'comprehensive visibility' (pattern 2), (4) abstract verbs — 'streamline', 'unlock', 'leverage', 'foster', 'implement' (pattern 1), (5) zero contractions (checklist step 4), (6) no questions or direct address (pattern 14), (7) no specific numbers or named mechanisms (pattern 10), (8) single long sentence with subordinate clauses rather than varied sentence lengths (pattern 9). The rewrite uses concrete verbs (ship, debug, page, deploy), breaks the sentence into varied lengths (short punch + longer clause), adds contractions where speech uses them, includes a question or direct address if tone permits, removes all nominalizations (verbs them), and names a specific mechanism or number. It follows the brand voice modifiers and avoids forbidden words.
